steps.title: Set up the mixture equation
steps.content:
Pure saline is conserved when mixing solutions. Identify the amount of pure saline in each component:
  • 3 liters at 10% contains $3 \times 0.10 = 0.3$ liters of pure saline
  • Let $x$ = liters of 25% solution to add, containing $0.25x$ liters of pure saline
  • Final mixture: $(3 + x)$ liters at 15% contains $0.15(3 + x)$ liters of pure saline

steps.title: Create the conservation equation
steps.content: Pure saline from both initial solutions must equal pure saline in the final mixture:

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Expand the right side:
$$0.3 + 0.25x = 0.45 + 0.15x$$
Subtract $0.15x$ from both sides and subtract $0.3$ from both sides:
$$0.10x = 0.15$$
Divide both sides by $0.10$:
$$x = 1.5$$
We must add 1.5 liters of the 25% saline solution.
